text:
Scarlet Eyes × Duel × Cost of Life (緋の眼×決闘×命の代償, Hi no Me × Kettō × Inochi no Daishō) is the 56 th episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime. It first aired on February 10 th , 2001. 

Overview

Killua's father was once paid to kill a Phantom Troupe member: he succeeded, but said it was hard and advised his sons to stay away from them. Gon, Killua, and Leorio search with no results. Gon gets a loan of 100 million by pawning his Hunter License. Kurapika and Uvogin fight, with Uvogin fighting at full strength, yet Kurapika has the upper hand due to a deadly oath and finally kills him.

Synopsis

Killua, Gon, and Leorio worry over Kurapika's safety, as he isn't answering their phone calls. Leorio tries to assure the other two boys that Kurapika is probably faring ok on his own. Still, Killua, in particular, worries about him potentially going up against the Phantom Troupe by himself. He recalled the time his father was tasked with targeting one of the members and complained about his opponent's strength, warning his sons not to go near the Troupe. The boys also worry about how they'll possibly be able to target the members of the Phantom Troupe themselves since they need the reward money.

Despite their concerns, the boys still decide to go after the Phantom Troupe members. Without being able to afford the cost of getting info on the Spiders from the Hunter Website, the boys decide to go off on their own to see if they can scout the Spiders out. The trio splits up, but they still have no leads on the Spiders by the time night falls.

Elsewhere, Uvogin and Kurapika prepare for their fight. Uvogin urinates to remove the last of the leeches from his body, while Kurapika says an oath under his breath to his fallen clan. When the two finally face each other, Uvogin asks Kurapika about his motivations for fighting him, and Kurapika tells him his intentions to get revenge for his clan. Uvogin, despite being able to recall some of the people he has killed in the past, cannot recall anything about the Kurta Clan or the Scarlet Eyes. When Kurapika questions Uvogin about how he feels when he kills people, he is enraged by Uvogin's nonchalant response, and their fight begins.

Uvogin and Kurapika can both dodge each other's initial attacks, but Uvogin can quickly land a punch on Kurapika. He is shocked to see that Kurapika seems completely uninjured from his attack. He rushes towards Kurapika, but Kurapika can easily dodge him and retaliate, getting behind Uvogin and kicking his head into the rock wall behind them. Uvogin gets serious and decides to start attacking Kurapika with his full power. He lunges at Kurapika and attacks a rock wall on the other side of them, which shakes a large boulder loose. When the boulder hits the ground, the area becomes clouded in sand and haze, and Kurapika cannot find Uvogin as he conceals his aura with In. Uvogin attacks Kurapika from behind, punching him to the ground. He assumes he has broken Kurapika's back with the amount of force he used, but to his complete shock, Kurapika stands back up.

Kurapika reveals that he had been using In to conceal his conjured chains wrapped around Uvogin's body. Uvogin is very confused, having assumed Kurapika to be a Manipulator. Kurapika removes one of his contacts to show his Scarlet Eyes and tells Uvogin that he is a Specialist when his eyes turn red. Uvogin, seeing the Scarlet Eyes before him, suddenly remembers seeing them previously on the Kurta Clan, remarking that Chrollo particularly liked them.

In a flashback to when Izunavi was training Kurapika, Izunavi notices Kurapika's aura suddenly strengthen as his eyes turn scarlet. When Kurapika's training has been completed, and Kurapika goes to leave, Izunavi warns him a final time to let go of his grudge. He claimed that Kurapika would gain nothing from it and could not handle the burden of having blood on his hands. 

In the present, Uvogin struggles to try and break out of Kurapika's chains while Kurapika heals the broken bones in his own arm completely. Kurapika explains that his middle chain enforces Zetsu on the Spiders trapped in it, suppressing their aura. Kurapika closes in on Uvogin and repeatedly punches him in the abdomen with his enhanced fist. He instructs Uvogin to tell him everything he knows about the Spiders. Still, Uvogin insists that Kurapika just kill him, refusing to give up any info on his fellow Troupe members. After a while of repeated beatings, Uvogin begins coughing up blood onto them both. Kurapika stabs into Uvogin's heart with his Stake of Retribution, giving him one last chance to reveal the location of the other Spiders. Uvogin, still refusing to tell Kurapika anything, gets his heart crushed by Kurapika's chain and falls forward, dead.

Immediately after the fight ends, Kurapika holds his own head, shaking violently. He digs a grave in the ground for Uvogin and buries his body.

Back in the city, the boys have yet to get a single lead on the Phantom Troupe, and Leorio suggests that they post an ad online looking for info on the Troupe, hoping that by asking enough follow-up questions, they'll be able to figure out who has legitimate leads. Gon suggests that they also offer a huge reward for the information. Because the trio doesn't have enough money, Gon offers to pawn his Hunter License.

At the Phantom Troupe's hideout, Shalnark worries about Uvogin. He checks in with Feitan first before going to Chrollo, who tells the Spiders that if Uvogin doesn't return to them by dawn, they'll shift their plans. 

Gon goes to a business to pawn his license and signs a contract in exchange for one hundred million. Although Killua and Leorio are unsure of the idea, Gon goes through with it.
